# Original email:

**Hello Umpire Registrants!**

I've been going through the umpire registrations and just wanted to point a few things out:

---

**1 - Volunteers are required to sign up for each volunteer role they undertake.**

If you've been allocated to a team under the umpire role, that was a mistake as umpires and board/key volunteers are NOT allocated to teams. If you've been allocated to a team in the umpire capacity, I have removed you from that team but your umpire registration remains valid and just as it should be.

For those who are volunteering in BOTH an umpire capacity AND Coach/Team Manager/Assistant Coach capacity, you need to have two separate registrations.

This is what something like that should look like (I will assign you to the team):

![Example of multiple registrations](https://ncllball.github.io/images/emails/umpire-signup/multi-registration.png)

If you are umpiring, volunteering as an assistant for your kids' A team and your other kids' AAA team, then you will need three separate registrations.

**Resolution**

Just sign up for another role as Coach/Team Manager/Assistant Coach capacity for each kids' team you want and I'll take it from there.

---

**2 - You only need to sign up for a single umpire role.**

The division you select DOES NOT matter and DOES NOT restrict you to games in that division — it's simply a registration quirk that requires an answer. Pick any division and move on. This would be incorrect.

![Example of incorrect multiple umpire role registrations](https://ncllball.github.io/images/emails/umpire-signup/single-umpire-role.png)

**Resolution**

I have removed many of these minor issues so I would like for everyone to check and make sure they like how their Volunteer registrations look.

---

**3 - If you plan to umpire in both Baseball and Softball, you must volunteer in both PROGRAMS.**

Baseball and Softball are considered separate in Sports Connect. Zachary Bennett is perfectly aligned for that.

![Example of Baseball and Softball program registrations](https://ncllball.github.io/images/emails/umpire-signup/baseball-softball-programs.png)

**Resolution**

Sign up for both programs, Softball and Baseball.

---

**Finally**

Having a parent/guardian/etc. on your team who is also an umpire is a great thing for a coach as it takes something very important off their plate. I coached from tee-ball to Majors and I was blessed to have Eric Evans in this role and it's a big deal.

That parent/umpire/etc. can confirm the team's games are covered and attempt to get coverage if not. If this is the case, it sounds to me like you are an assistant coach in charge of umpiring for the team so sign up to be an assistant!

Thanks and please let me know any issues or questions you have!

John Shikella  
Registrar/Information
# Response from the UIC for NCLL (Umpire in Chief)

Jon Becker
2:19 PM (1 hour ago)
to me, Ump

Hi John,

Can I get some clarification around point #3? If an umpire is registered as an umpire for baseball, then they are a registered volunteer with the league, they've had a background check, etc. Why is it necessary to have a separate registration for baseball and softball, but not a separate one for each division? Is there anything which prevents them from also umpiring softball?

I would dearly like to see a report which quickly tells me how many distinct umpires I have, without having to see any duplicate entries because of multiple divisions (which you've fixed) or because of having to register for both baseball and softball. And mostly, I would like individual umpires who have signed up expecting to only do baseball, but then decide they might want to give softball a try (or vice versa) to have no impediments to this from an administrative standpoint.

-Jon

# My Response:

Hey Jon, I couldn't agree more — let me go into some detail here in hopes we can come up with something.

Just so we're on the same page with my terminology:

- **Program** = Sport/activity — Baseball, Basketball, Football, Soccer, Softball, Volleyball, Wrestling, Track and Field, Cross Country, Dodgeball, Rugby
- **Division** = TB / KCP / A / AA / AAA / MAJ / JUN / SEN
- **Team** = Team

I always think the best analogy for Sports Connect and its limitations in a case like this is a Boys and Girls Club that offers multiple different sports.

When I create a new registration, the first question I get is to choose one single PROGRAM (sport), then it asks if I want it to be affiliated with the national level organization — LLI in our case. If I choose yes, this applies the different divisions (A/AA/AAA, etc.) with a rough framework of ages, birthdates, schools, address, etc. that essentially define our NCLL geographic area and offerings for that PROGRAM. This includes certain questions, requirements, etc. for families to work through.

Doing this, as you would probably expect since we affiliated with the national level, severely cuts down the amount of leeway we have in most areas. We have a little leeway for ages that define a division, names of the division (like A/AA vs. calling it Minors), but even the number of divisions in a PROGRAM is capped at a very low number and we use all of them.

If I were to choose not to join the national level, there would be no automatic compliance reports, no waiving of background check fees, etc., that come from LLI. This is fine for winter training with 50 kids (and it is exactly what we use it for). It would essentially mean we would be on the hook for all reporting, compliance, and everything we agree to within the charter, but with none of the help from LLI. We could just have one PROGRAM with SB and BB Divisions and we could create as many divisions as we wanted. But that isn't practical because of the amount of work.

Here are a couple of square pegs I've tried to fit into round holes — workarounds that stay within the national level organization rules but carve something different out:

- create a PROGRAM for umpires
- designate a division within a PROGRAM as Umpires

These fall apart pretty quickly due to the reasons above. It's here where the break between Softball and Baseball begins, just as dodgeball and rugby are different from one another. Simply put, I cannot choose a PROGRAM that can house both seamed ball sports nor can I create one.

So to bring it down to a practical level, I'll ask and answer a question that may help. What happens if I sign Jasper up for Juniors SOFTBALL by mistake? It happens a handful of times per year and I need to transfer their order from one PROGRAM to the other. Again, this would be just like transferring from Rugby to Dodgeball. However, this is really just the equivalent of creating a new registration for that child in the PROGRAM they are being transferred into and removing them from the previous. This extends to Volunteers and hence, Umpires.

One caveat I'll mention is that when I claim — as I did in my email — that you CANNOT be allocated to a team with the role Umpire or Board/Key Volunteer, that is a limit imposed by me — not technically. It can be done, and done very easily. I have just chosen not to for these reasons (and these apply to Board/Key Volunteers as well):

- I think showing up to a game unaffiliated with a team vs. on a team's roster listed as an Umpire is a better look for independence. I think it puts the umpires in a different headspace with regard to independence too.
- The umpire role is treated differently than coaches — communication and emails are routed differently in the system, which would create more confusion than it solves. To make the umpire list meaningful, keeping them off team rosters is essential.
- I had to choose one way or the other and I think it's a no brainer to go with not assigning Umpires to teams. There is literally no upside to it that I know of.

I've always assumed the same firewall between PROGRAMS continues downstream to something like Stack Officials. The reasons I ask for it explicitly, as I do in point #3, are essentially:

- to cover all of our bases for the umpires so there are no surprises later
- to streamline reporting and keep the umpire list clean
- to maintain consistency across both PROGRAMS

A few options I'm thinking about:

(I should say that I can be a stickler for the rules, but I believe this is such a technicality that I have NO problem with anyone who is registered as an umpire in either Baseball or Softball signing up to umpire in EITHER PROGRAM while only registered to one. As Registrar, I wouldn't force umpires in one PROGRAM working games in the other to register as an umpire in that PROGRAM.)

- Make it universal — pick one PROGRAM, Baseball or Softball, and we enforce it as a standard. Just like not assigning umpires to teams, we set the rule and it's easily enforceable.
- Reword point #3 in the original email to better reflect the reality and reduce confusion.
- I'd love to better understand what your view looks like on the Stack Officials side?

I'm not sure if you saw Jason's email earlier in the week, but more changes are coming from LLI and Sports Connect, so working too hard on this for next year at this point is silly. That said, the email I sent out today to the umpires is the basis for a note in next year's registration to help make it more clear.
